description: A clean White House data-visualization infographic (line chart) on a white background contrasting the Biden and Trump administrations on native-born employment. The serif title reads 'Total Native-Born Employment Level' (with 'Employment Level' in italic). A two-item legend uses a navy dot for 'Biden Administration' and a red dot for 'Trump Administration (so far)'. The y-axis is labeled 'Total employment (thousands)' running roughly 129,000 to 134,000; the x-axis spans Nov. 2023 through Nov. 2025 in three-month increments. A dashed vertical gridline near Jan/Feb 2025 is labeled 'Inauguration'. The line is colored navy for the Biden-era portion (showing a dip and roughly flat/stagnant native-born employment around 130,000-132,000) and switches to red after inauguration, rising steadily upward to about 133,000+ by late 2025. A dark navy footer bar carries 'THE WHITE HOUSE / WASHINGTON'. The messaging technique uses a color-coded before/after chart to credit Trump with rising native-born employment versus a flat Biden trend (the tweet argues foreign-born workers took 'ALL net job growth in 2024'). Target audience: economically focused voters and an 'American-born workers first' / immigration-restrictionist audience. This is the first of two chart images in the same tweet.
notable_text: Total Native-Born Employment Level. Biden Administration. Trump Administration (so far). Inauguration. THE WHITE HOUSE WASHINGTON
